summary: Added a visual Creator Kit panel that turns the standalone repo extension path into five build gates for adding lanes, visuals, minigames, bot ownership, and publishable template shape.

queries: developer onboarding checklist empty state product UI best practices
GitHub template repository onboarding checklist documentation best practices
progressive disclosure onboarding dashboard setup checklist UX
pattern_notes: Template repositories should preserve a reusable directory and file shape that others can start from.
Onboarding should show the next useful action rather than leaving new users in a blank or documentation-only state.
Progressive disclosure supports complex setup flows by turning broad instructions into staged gates.
Dashboard setup panels should expose actionable hierarchy and live progress rather than only explanatory copy.

ui_contract: Render Creator Kit below Expansion Forge.
Show five setup gates: seed a lane, forge visual identity, wire a minigame, bind the bot, and ship the template.
Each gate lists the core files or folders a forked repo owner should touch.
Each gate includes a progress charge, unlock description, and related Atlas action.
FORGE scrolls to Expansion Forge, ART opens the Visual Asset Vault, PATH opens selected lane Path Map, COM opens selected lane command deck, and FEED scrolls to Mission Feed.
The panel is static and local-only; it does not create lanes, send commands, or mutate data.

checks: 390x920 viewport: Creator Kit rendered 5 cards in 1 column with no horizontal overflow, no console errors, and no failed responses.
1280x800 viewport: Creator Kit rendered 5 cards in 5 columns, 5 action buttons, file chips, and a 99% setup score with no horizontal overflow.
1280x800 interaction: PATH changed the hash to #lane=platform_engineering&view=path and rendered Path Map.
1280x800 interaction: COM changed the hash to #lane=platform_engineering&view=comms and rendered the command deck.
1280x800 interaction: ART opened the Visual Asset Vault with 41 assets visible.
1280x800 interaction: FEED set Mission Feed to all and scrolled to the feed with 90 events visible.
1600x900 viewport: Creator Kit rendered 5 cards in 5 columns with no horizontal overflow, no console errors, and no failed responses.
